user_message: Give me the security posture for my team. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 16:33 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 16:25 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 17:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-02-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is security posture.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
